title: eriosema benguellense
intro: Eriosema benguellense is a plant species belonging to the Fabaceae family, commonly known as a legume or pea family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, malaria, and other diseases caused by parasites. The plant is believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Eriosema benguellense can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional medicine, particularly in African cultures.
